Fosters possibilities to communicate across disciplines and engage with current affairs through news segment, radio and tv podcasts and other media. Topics may include ecology, climate change, the environment, geopolitics, global fashion industry, health, intercultural exchanges, music.

title: FRE 4420 Writing in French 3 Credits
description: Advanced writing course that provides a systematic study (or review) of French syntax, vocabulary and style with the help of drill sessions. Also may include some training in literary translation. A number of quizzes and written compositions.

title: FRE 4501 The French Language in the Americas 3 Credits
description: Examination of the presence of French in the Americas. Topics covered include: the origin of French in the Americas, language practices of Francophone communities, linguistic characteristics of the varieties of French, the effect of language contact on language behavior and representations and identity issues. Focus varies from year to year.

title: FRE 4780 Introduction to French Phonetics and Phonology 3 Credits
description: An introduction to French phonological processes, providing explanatory evidence for the production of speech sounds, for the classification of sounds, for their interrelationship with one another (gliding, nasalization, assimilation), for morphological and syllable structure, for specifically French phenomena such as liaison, elision, final consonant drop, schwa drop, and for the relationship of morphology to phonology, especially in the verb system.

title: FRE 4822 Sociolinguistics of French 3 Credits
description: Sociolinguistic issues in the French-speaking world: language variation, discourse analysis, attitudes toward varieties of French and contact with speakers of other languages.

title: FRE 4850 Introduction to the Structure of French 3 Credits
description: Explores the French language as a system of communication and mental representation. Analyzes the morphological, syntactic and semantic aspects of contemporary French, and emphasizes the historical, psychological and sociological dimension of linguistic investigation.

title: FRE 4905 Individual Work 1-4 Credits
description: For advanced major and minors who seek independent work not offered in another course. Must be arranged individually with French faculty.

title: FRE 4906 Honors Thesis 1-3 Credits
description: Directed research leading to a 30-40 page essay on a topic approved by the thesis director; registration for two semesters is highly recommended. Not a substitute for a required course in the French major.

title: FRE 4911 Undergraduate Research in Language or Linguistics 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Language or Linguistics. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Language or Linguistics.

title: FRE 4930 Revolving Topics in French Studies 1-5 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in French studies.

title: FRE 4956 Overseas Studies 1-18 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: FRT 2460 French Texts and Contexts 3 Credits
description: Selected readings in English translation of major works of French literature. For those with no knowledge of French.

title: FRT 2930 Special Topics in French Literature and Culture 3 Credits
description: Rotating topics in French literature and culture; taught in English.

title: FRT 3004 Monuments and Masterpieces of France 3 Credits
description: Study of selected masterpieces of French literature, in English translation. Works to be considered as they relate to history and as they can be read in strictly literary terms. Topic varies from year to year.

title: FRT 3520 French Cinema 4-8 Credits
description: Critical, theoretical, and historical study of French cinema. Content may include key directors, 1930s cinema, nostalgia, and masculinity in 1980s films, World War II cinema, and Colonial and Postcolonial cinema. Taught in English.

title: FRT 3561 Women in French Literature and/or Cinema 3-4 Credits
description: Introduction to the rich heritage of feminist traditions in France and Francophone countries through an exploration of women writers and thinkers (filmmakers, theorists), primarily of the 19th and 20th centuries. Selected topics include L'Écriture féminine (Writing the Feminine), autobiographical writing by French and Francophone women, women in French cinema, and representations of women in French film and literature. Read, discuss, and analyze a broad spectrum of primary and secondary sources from a feminist viewpoint.

title: FRT 4523 European Identities, European Cinemas 4 Credits
description: Provides knowledge of different cultures, languages, and identities that make up contemporary European cinemas.

title: FRT 4911 Undergraduate Research in English Translation 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in English Translation. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in English Translation.

title: FRT 4956 Overseas Studies 3 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: FRW 3100 Introduction to French Literature 1 3 Credits
description: Overview of French Medieval, Renaissance and classical literature and culture, with major literary, intellectual and historical trends through study of representative works from each period. Emphasizes close reading of texts to train students to read critically and to familiarize them with major authors, genres and interpretations. (N)

title: FRW 3101 Introduction to French Literature 2 3 Credits
description: Selected readings of outstanding authors of prose fiction, poetry and theatre from the 18th to the 20th century. Provides the historical context for major literary movements and authors, and trains students to read and write critically. Generally organized thematically. (N)

title: FRW 3930 Rotating Topics in French and Francophone Literature 3 Credits
description: Selected topics in French literature.

title: FRW 4212 Readings in 17th Century French Prose 3 Credits
description: Selected readings with an emphasis on the history of ideas, the moralistes and culture in the early modern period. Texts include Descartes, Cyrano de Bergerac, Pascal, Fontenelle, La Rochefoucauld, La Fayette, La Bruyère, and Svign.

title: FRW 4273 Readings in 18th Century French Literature 3 Credits
description: Rotating topics exploring the fiction, theatre or intellectual prose of the Enlightenment. Special emphasis placed on the cultural climate and productions of the ancient regime.

title: FRW 4281 Readings in the 20th Century French Novel 3 Credits
description: Examination of representative novels in 20th century French literature from Proust to the New Novel and beyond. Emphasis may include study of genre, narrative techniques, literary modernism and major themes. Combines an historical approach with close textual readings. Authors frequently studied include Proust, Gide, Malraux, Cline, Camus, Sartre, Robbe-Grillet, Butor, Sarraute and Duras.

title: FRW 4310 Seventeenth Century French Drama 3 Credits
description: Theory and practice of dramaturgy in the classical period as reflected in plays of Corneille, Molire, and Racine. Close textual analysis to disengage aesthetic and ideological problematics posed by each play.

title: FRW 4324 Readings in 20th and 21st c. French theater 3 Credits
description: A study of selected plays (by Anouilh, Beckett, Césaire, Duras, Genet, Ionesco, Jarry, Réza, Sarraute, Sartre, etc.), dramatic techniques and stage performances in 20th and 21st century French and Francophone theater.

title: FRW 4350 Modern French Poetry from Baudelaire to the Present 3 Credits
description: Combines an historical approach with close readings of poetic texts; also introduces a number of theoretical and critical writings. Although traditional poetic texts are studied, the works of less frequently taught poets are also presented.

title: FRW 4391 Concepts of French Cinema 4 Credits
description: A critical and historical study of the representation of gender and ethnicity in French cinema.

title: FRW 4532 Survey of French Romantic Literature 3 Credits
description: A rotating topics tracing the development and the main tenets of 19th century French Romanticism. Concentrates on various themes and genres, including poetry, theater and the novel as well as the socioeconomic and cultural matrices that fostered the movement. Emphasizes the relationship between literature and the visual arts, constructions of gendered, cultural and artistic subjectivities, exoticism (spatial, temporal and mystical voyages) and representations of Paris and French society. Beginning with pre-Romantic authors, moves on to Lamartine, Stendhal, Hugo, Vigny, Balzac, Sand, Musset, Desbordes-Valmore, Nerval and Baudelaire.

title: FRW 4552 Introduction to Realism and Naturalism 3 Credits
description: Rotating topics trace the development and the main tenets of latter 19th century literary, artistic, and cultural productions. Concentrates on various themes and genres, including poetry, theatre, and the novel as well as the socioeconomic and cultural matrices that fostered movements arising between 1850 and the Fin de siècle. Specific attention may be devoted to definitions of Realism, Naturalism, and Symbolism; representations of modern life and the industrialization of the literary market; the infiltration of scientific and mechanistic thought into literary productions (poetry or prose); and representations of women and the female body, of Paris and Parisian society.

title: FRW 4762 Readings in Francophone Literatures and Cultures (excluding the Caribbean and Sub-Saharan Africa) 3 Credits
description: Rotating topics in the literatures and cultures of the Francophone world, including North America (Quebec), Europe (Belgium, Switzerland and regional France), Asia (Vietnam, Indian Ocean) and the Middle East.

title: FRW 4770 African and Caribbean Literatures 3 Credits
description: The production of Sub-Saharan African writers from its inception to the present through examination of representative works and figures, genres (epics, poetry, drama, novels), discourses and critics that inform the productions. Particular attention is paid to historical, political and cultural issues that figure in the development and orientation of African literature in French. Occasional inclusion of Caribbean literature for purpose of comparison.

title: FRW 4822 Introduction to French Critical Theory 3 Credits
description: Review and comparative analysis of approaches to literature from Romanticism to Deconstructionism. Reading and writing are examined through the eyes of Sainte-Beuve, Taine, Lanson, Bachelard, the Geneva School, Ricoeur, Bataille, Blanchot, Barthes, Foucault, Genette, Lacan, Kristeva, Todorov, Derrida and others.

title: FRW 4911 Undergraduate Research in Target Language 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Target Language. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Target Language.

title: FRW 4932 Senior Seminar in French Literature 3 Credits
description: Rotating topics for French majors and minors in the senior year. Topics rotate given research interests or field of specialization of the teaching faculty. Recent topics have included La Posie du Voyage; L'Écriture féminine; Le Discontinu chez La Rochefoucauld et La Bruyre; Charles Baudelaire: pote/critique de la vie moderne; Qu'est-ce que la Rvolte?

title: GER 1125 Discover German 1 5 Credits
description: First semester of a two-semester sequence that includes Discover German 2. In this innovative online course, students acquire basic skills in the German language and use the Internet as a resource to explore aspects of German culture and everyday life.

title: GER 1126 Discover German 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of Discover German 1. Continue acquiring basic skills in the German language using the Internet to explore aspects of German culture and everyday life.

title: GER 1130 Beginning German 1 5 Credits
description: First of a two-semester Beginning German sequence. Focuses on development of CEFR A1-level proficiency in reading, listening, speaking and writing. Enhancement of digital-literacy skills through use of online resources including generative AI for production of interactive simulations of everyday life in German-speaking contexts.

title: GER 1131 Beginning German 2 5 Credits
description: Second of a two-semester Beginning German sequence. Focuses on development of CEFR A2-level proficiency in reading, listening, speaking and writing. Enhancement of digital-literacy skills through use of online resources including generative AI for production of interactive simulations of everyday life in German-speaking contexts.

title: GER 2200 Intermediate German 1 3 Credits
description: Participants will improve their skills in the four basic areas (reading, writing, listening comprehension, speaking) by reviewing elements of grammar, particularly morphology (i.e., word forms), and by expanding vocabulary. Upon successful completion, students may go directly to Advanced German.

title: GER 2225 Online Intermediate German I 3 Credits
description: Employs web-based learning resources to develop B1-level proficiencies via the reinforcement of speaking and listening comprehension, reading, and writing skills, and an exploration of selected cultural topics. For self-starters who are comfortable and successful working individually and in groups in an online learning environment.

title: GER 2226 Online Intermediate German II 3 Credits
description: Develops language comprehension and production skills in work on German texts with various topics (historical, scientific, medical, artistic, etc.) and via online writing tasks and speaking exercises in biweekly synchronous student-instructor meetings.

title: GER 2240 Intermediate German 2 3 Credits
description: Objectives include improving reading and speaking skills at the intermediate level. Upon completion participants will be able to pronounce German words more accurately, listen with greater comprehension to German and respond to questions about a variety of recorded texts. Upon successful completion, students may go directly to Advanced German.

title: GER 2270 Intermediate German Abroad 3-9 Credits
description: A review of the major aspects of grammar in a context that enhances understanding of German and aims at a level of proficiency above the A2 level of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.

title: GER 3234 Reading German Texts 3 Credits
description: Upon completion, students will be able to read literary and nonliterary texts and to identify the more common syntactical, stylistic and rhetorical elements.

title: GER 3300 Writing German Texts 3 Credits
description: Special focus on strategies, grammar and vocabulary involved in writing in German. Works toward a writing proficiency level that corresponds to the B1 level as defined by the European Framework of Reference for Languages and measured by the Goethe Institute exam Zertifikat Deutsch.

title: GER 3330 German Language and Culture 1 3 Credits
description: Introduces German civilization through grammar review, vocabulary building, reading and essay writing. Upon completion, students will be able to discuss cultural and literary concepts in German.

title: GER 3332 Topics in German Film and Culture 1 Credit
description: GER 3224 is taught as a FLAC accompaniment to various courses. A discussion forum covering different topics in German film and culture. All materials and class discussions will be in German.

title: GER 3401 German Grammar Review 3 Credits
description: An intensive semester-long review of German grammar.

title: GER 3413 German Listening, Comprehension and Speaking 3 Credits
description: Develops the ability to understand and produce basic kinds of speech (descriptions, simple stories, etc.).

title: GER 3440 German in Business 3 Credits
description: Study of Wirtschaftsdeutsch and the ability to read and write texts in German for international business transactions. Acquire the ability to communicate in German in professional business settings.

title: GER 3470 Advanced German Abroad 3-9 Credits
description: Practical, in-class communication exercises at an advanced level in comprehension, speaking, reading and writing. Prepares students to achieve a proficiency level above the B1 level as established by the Common European Framework for Languages.

title: GER 4482 Cultural Identity and Intercultural Competence 3 Credits
description: Listening comprehension and speaking ability in work involving German cultural identity, online and in class.

title: GER 4930 Variable Topics in German Studies 3 Credits
description: Working with German media such as major newspapers, magazines, radio and television programs, students will refine their language skills to a level similar to the B2 level of the Common European Framework for Languages.

title: GER 4956 Overseas Studies 1-15 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: GET 2100 German Literary Heritage 3 Credits
description: Upon completion participants will be able to discuss major works of German literature, mainly from the 18th to the 20th century. Texts and lectures in English. Knowledge of German is not required.

title: GET 3003 German Culture and Civilization 1 3 Credits
description: Knowledge of German is not required. Introduces German civilization from the earliest times to the beginning of the 19th century. Become acquainted with German literature, philosophy, art, music, and architecture. Taught in English.

title: GET 3004 Modern German Culture and Civilization 3 Credits
description: Knowledge of German is not required. Introduces German civilization in the 19th and 20th centuries. Discuss developments in German literature, philosophy, art, music, and architecture. Taught in English.

title: GET 3200 Medieval Literary Culture 3 Credits
description: Studies the German literary culture of the Holy Roman Empire during the Middle Ages in its broader European context. Examines literary culture in relation to Latin literary traditions, as well as to other cultural domains such as the arts, architecture, theology, and politics.

title: GET 3201 Early Modern Literary Culture 3 Credits
description: Studies the early modern literary culture of German-speaking regions of the Holy Roman Empire in the broader European context. Examines this culture’s relation to pan-European developments, including the Renaissance, Humanism, the Reformation, and developments in the arts, architecture, theology, and politics.

title: GET 3501 History, Literature and Arts of Berlin 3 Credits
description: Gain a working knowledge of the history and culture of Berlin from 1871 to the present by exploring literature, painting, and film. Authors and artists include Mann, Brecht, Dix, and Wenders.

title: GET 3520 Early German Cinema to 1945 4 Credits
description: Historical overview of the most influential films of German classical cinema, including how they relate to the social reality of the 1920s and 30s.

title: GET 3580 Representations of War in Literature and Visual Media 3 Credits
description: Study of the shifting cultural representations of war (literature/art) in the 20th century, focusing primarily on European history, culture and politics. Primary sources include Mann, Remarque, Boll, Celan, Grosz, and Kiefer.

title: GET 3930 Variable Topics in German Studies 3-9 Credits
description: Variable topics in German Studies in English translation.

title: GET 4521 Women and German Cinema 4 Credits
description: Women and German Cinema

title: GET 4523 New Cinema 1945 to the Present 4 Credits
description: New Cinema 1945 to the Present

title: GET 4930 Variable Topics in German Studies 3-9 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in German studies, in English translation.

title: GEW 3930 Variable Topics in German Studies 3-9 Credits
description: Variable topics in German Studies.

title: GEW 4301 Introduction to German Drama and Theater 3 Credits
description: Survey of German drama from the late medieval period to the present. Introduction to major forms, periods and authors such as Lessing, Goethe, Schiller, and Brecht.

title: GEW 4401 Cities as Cultural Centers 3 Credits
description: Study of German, Austrian, and Swiss cities as cultural centers. Employs online learning resources to expand knowledge of urban cultures in German-speaking Europe and to develop linguistic and cultural proficiency toward the B2 level according to CEFR standards.

title: GEW 4730 Modern German Literature 3 Credits
description: Major trends of German literature in the first half of the 20th century, including impressionism, expressionism, Neue Sachlichkeit, workers literature, and Nazi culture. Discusses works by Thomas Mann, Hermann Hesse, and Bertolt Brecht.

title: GEW 4731 Contemporary German Literature 3 Credits
description: Learn to discern the major trends of post WW II German literature and recognize the key features of works by such German authors as Böll, Grass, Weiss, and Wolf, and by such Swiss and Austrian writers as Frisch, Dürrenmatt, and Handke.

title: GEW 4750 Women in German Literature 3 Credits
description: The image of women in representative works of German literature. Study of the main themes and structures in selected works by major women writers of German-speaking countries and their unique contribution to German culture and literature. Students will use an interdisciplinary approach to literature and will learn the basic tools of gender studies.

title: GEW 4760 Communities Writing in German 3 Credits
description: Writings (prose, poetry, critical essays) of significant authors of non-German descent (i.e., Spaniards, Italians, Turks, Iranians) from the 1950s to the present. Insights into the lives and thinking of significant ethnic minorities in Germany during this period and the implications for German literary history and German identity. Films are also screened and discussed.

title: GEW 4905 Individual Work 1-3 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course. Available only by special arrangement.

title: GEW 4911 Undergraduate Research in German 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in German. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in German.

title: GEW 4930 Seminar in Germanic Languages and Literatures 3 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in Germanic language or literature.

title: MEM 2500 The Tales of King Arthur 3 Credits
description: Upon completion students will be familiar with some of the great literary works of the Arthurian tradition, and with the manner in which the tales of King Arthur serve, from their sixth century Celtic origins throughout the Western canon, to articulate the interests and values of different social groups through history.

title: MEM 3300 Castles and Cloisters: An Introduction to Medieval Communities 3 Credits
description: Studies monastic and courtly-chivalric communities as these evolved in the Middle Ages. Explores architecture, art, literature, and music illustrate how different monastic and chivalric communities saw the world and their place in it.

title: HAI 1130 Beginning Haitian Creole 1 5 Credits
description: HAI 1130 and its sequel, HAI 1131, constitute the basic sequence for development of conversational skills and grammar essentials in the language.

title: HAI 1131 Beginning Haitian Creole 2 5 Credits
description: Second part of the basic Haitian Creole sequence for development of conversational skills and grammar essentials in the language.

title: HAI 2200 Intermediate Haitian Creole 1 3 Credits
description: Concentrates on conversation and readings and provides an introduction to Haitian culture through music and film.

title: HAI 2201 Intermediate Haitian Creole 2 3 Credits
description: Continued concentration on conversation with added emphasis on reading and perspectives on issues related to the Haitian way of living.

title: HAI 3930 Topics in Haitian Language and Culture 3 Credits
description: Variable topics in Haitian language, culture, and society.

title: HAI 4905 Individual Work 1-4 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course. Must be arranged individually with Haitian faculty.

title: HAI 4911 Undergraduate Research in Haitian Creole 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in language, linguistics, literature, culture in Haitian Creole. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: HAT 3503 Haitian Culture and Literature in Translation 3 Credits
description: Examines representations of Haiti and its culture through Haitian literature, art, film, and music.

title: HAT 3564 Haitian Culture and Society 3 Credits
description: Addresses central aspects of history, politics, environment, and development, including attention to gender relations, medicine, education, work, race, and class. No knowledge of Haitian Creole is required.

title: HAT 3700 Introduction to Haitian Creole Linguistics 3 Credits
description: Examines the major sub-fields of linguistics by means of the Haitian Creole language. Introduces Haitian Creole syntax, morphology, phonology, semantics, and lexicon, as well as sociolinguistics, dialectology, language planning, bilingualism, and language contact.

title: HAT 4911 Undergraduate Research in English, Haitian Creole or French 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application in some combination of English, Haitian Creole or French.

title: JMT 3500 Jamaican Creole, Reggae, and Rastafari 3 Credits
description: Studies Jamaican Creole, reggae music, and Rastafari religion and culture; introduces the language, music, and religion of the Caribbean island. Methodology includes linguistics, ethnomusicology, and religious studies to read Jamaican Creole, interpret reggae songs, and analyze the Rastafari culture to which they link.

title: HBT 3223 Identity and Dissent in the Hebrew Short Story 3 Credits
description: Traces the tension between the individual and the collective in Zionist/Israeli society over the last 100-plus years as illustrated in Hebrew short fiction.

title: HIN 4930 Special Topics in Hindi-Urdu 3 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in and in-depth study of prose or poetic genres in Hindi and/or Urdu. Supplementary critical readings in English.

title: ITA 1130 Beginning Italian 1 5 Credits
description: ITA 1130 and its sequel, ITA 1131, constitute the basic sequence in Italian. Emphasis on the development of broad competence in the language. Oral-aural approach ensures competence in the four skills.

title: ITA 1131 Beginning Italian 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of the basic sequence in Italian. Emphasis on the development of broad competence in the language. Oral-aural approach ensures competence in the four skills.

title: ITA 2220 Intermediate Italian 1 4 Credits
description: Enhances knowledge of Italian in all four skills: listening, reading, speaking, and writing. The goal is to create communicative competence that enables advancement to third year study and to benefit from Italy visits.

title: ITA 2221 Intermediate Italian 2 4 Credits
description: Completes second year sequence with emphasis on composition, literature and communication skills.

title: ITA 3070 Accelerated Introduction to Italian 5 Credits
description: Intensive course designed primarily for speakers or students with knowledge of another Romance language or strong linguistic abilities in another foreign language. Assumes no prior study of Italian and offers a complete four-skill (listening, speaking, reading and writing) introduction to the language.

title: ITA 3224 Italian Enhancement Section 1-5 Credits
description: Italian-language reading and discussion to accompany and complement courses in other departments. Readings and discussions are in Italian to develop vocabulary and fluency related to the content of the companion course and to provide an international perspective on the issues of the main course.

title: ITA 3243 Italian Conversation 3 Credits
description: Develops and refines Italian oral and comprehension skills through exposure to and discussion of a range of topics relating to contemporary Italian society. Discussion and assignments address different registers of speech, from the colloquial to the formal. Active in-class participation is essential.

title: ITA 3420 Grammar and Composition 1 3 Credits
description: Intensive language course designed to master grammatical principles, increase vocabulary and enhance writing and composition skills.

title: ITA 3500 Italian Civilization 3 Credits
description: Variable topics introduce Italian civilization in historical, artistic, and literary contexts.

title: ITA 3503 Naples: Culture and History 3 Credits
description: Historical, cultural, and socio-political exploration of the Italian city of Naples from its foundation by the Greeks to its present-day position within the Italian State. Readings, discussion, and analysis encompass the city’s rich literary, artistic, architectural, theatrical, and musical traditions as well as the socio-political history of Naples and the region of Campania.

title: ITA 3564 Contemporary Italian Culture 3 Credits
description: Variable topics focus on modern Italy through literature, art and mass media, and current events.

title: ITA 4905 Individual Work 1-4 Credits
description: For advanced minors who seek independent work not offered in another course. Must be arranged individually with Italian faculty. Only three credits can count toward the minor.

title: ITA 4911 Undergraduate Research in Language or History/Culture in Italian 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: ITT 2530 Italian Literature and Film 3 Credits
description: Study of modern Italian narrative and its adaptation to film by major Italian directors.

title: ITT 3431 Italy and Pilgrimages 3 Credits
description: Through lectures, readings, and discussions, considers the continuing presence of Rome and other Italian cities as metaphors and focal points of Italian artistic and literary sensibilities. Taught in English.

title: ITT 3521 Italian Cinema 4 Credits
description: Critical and historical study of Italian film and directors. Topics may vary.

title: ITT 3540 Murder Italian Style: Crime Fiction and Film in Italy 3 Credits
description: Explores Italian crime fiction and film. Analysis considers specificity of Italian tradition while placing texts and films in a broader cultural and historical context, enabling critically and politically informed reading of the many applications and subversions of the generic formula. Taught in English.

title: ITT 3541 Gangsters and Godfathers: Italian Mafia Movies 3 Credits
description: Examines Italian cinematic representations of organized criminality, in particular Sicilian Cosa Nostra and Neapolitan Camorra. Analysis considers specificity of Italian tradition while placing films in a broader history of the gangster genre to allow for a critically and politically informed reading of Italian generic subversions. Taught in English.

title: ITT 3700 The Demolition of Man: Italian Perspectives on the Jewish Holocaust 3 Credits
description: Explores a sampling of Italy's texts on the Jewish Holocaust, centering on the work of survivor and theorist of the camps, Primo Levi. Material also encompasses the history of the Italian-Jewish community, as well as a selection of Italian films dealing with the realities and ethical implications of the camps.

title: ITT 3930 Special Topics in Italian Literature and Culture 3 Credits
description: Selected topics in Italian literature, civilization and culture, including crossover influence of media. Can focus on one epoch's influence on another.

title: ITT 4911 Undergraduate Research in Italian in English Translation 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: ITT 4956 Overseas Studies 1-18 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: ITW 3100 Introduction to Italian Literature 1 3 Credits
description: The origins of early Italian literature, its central themes, and the cultural factors that influenced its development. Develops an ability to read critically in Italian.

title: ITW 3101 Introduction to Italian Literature 2 3-9 Credits
description: The major Italian authors from the Renaissance through the 20th century.

title: ITW 3310 Italian Play 3 Credits
description: Italian Play

title: ITW 3500 Boccaccio's Decameron 3 Credits
description: Analysis of Giovanni Boccaccio’s canonical Decameron with a focus on the literary Dimension of Text as well as the political, historical, and religious context in which the work was written.

title: ITW 4026C Representing the Humble Italy: Literature and Cinema of the Italian South 3 Credits
description: Examines texts and films that address the so-called Southern Question; namely, the socioeconomic and cultural disparities between northern and southern Italy. Explores or contests topics and concepts including southern exclusion from official history, southern fusion of religion and superstition, resistance to modernity and mafia-related political corruption. Taught in Italian.

title: ITW 4253 Delitto all'italiana: Crime Fiction and Film in Italy 3 Credits
description: Explores a sampling of Italian crime fiction and film through the lens of a range of conceptual categories, including British intellectual model vs. American noir; rational inquiry vs. uncertainties of sensory perception; canon vs. popular; cosmopolitan vs. provincial; order vs. chaos; political conservatism vs. social critique. Taught in Italian.

title: ITW 4484 Modern Italian Novel 3 Credits
description: Critical exploration of the modern Italian novel, focusing on representative texts of the long 20th century. Development of Italian novel is analyzed in context of technological modernity, war, fascism, economic boom. Issues of literary technique and formal innovation also considered. Novels may vary from semester to semester.

title: ITW 4491 Italian Theater from the Renaissance to the Early Modern Era 3 Credits
description: Overview and analysis of evolution of Italian theater, with focus on a selection of specific examples drawn from Italian theater from the Renaissance to the early modern era. Taught in Italian.

title: ITW 4526 Mad Love in Modern Italian Literature 3 Credits
description: Exploration of a sampling of modern Italian literary manifestations of a love that strays beyond the conventional. Language skills addressed through discussion, oral presentations, creative writing assignments, and short papers.

title: ITW 4580 Animals and Animality in Italian Culture 3 Credits
description: Presentation and exploration of the figure of the nonhuman animal in the work of several modern Italian authors and poets. Analysis addresses the literary dimension of the selected animal portraits as well as any philosophical and ethical questions raised by these literary animals. Taught in Italian.

title: ITW 4600 Dante's Inferno 3 Credits
description: Semester-long, in-depth examination of Dante Alighieri's text, Inferno, with the support of a variety of visual materials and digital resources devoted to Dante and his world. Special attention paid to the political, historical, and religious context in which Dante wrote. Taught in Italian.

title: ITW 4911 Undergraduate Research in Literature in Italian 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Literature in Italian. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Literature in Italian.

title: JPN 1130 Beginning Japanese 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering four skills: listening, speaking, reading, and writing. A placement test is required for students with any prior background in Japanese. Complete beginners may register without a placement test.

title: JPN 1131 Beginning Japanese 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: JPN 2230 Intermediate Japanese 1 5 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: JPN 2231 Intermediate Japanese 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of intermediate study.

title: JPN 3410 Advanced Japanese 1 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: JPN 3411 Advanced Japanese 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of advanced study in Japanese.

title: JPN 3440 Business Japanese 3 Credits
description: Provides grammatical structures and essential business vocabulary, develops conversation strategies and presentation skills, and raises awareness of the customs and cultural differences in Japanese business interactions.

title: JPN 3730 Language in Japanese Society 3 Credits
description: Analysis of variation in regional dialects: gender-based differences, pragmatics of interpersonal communication, language acquisition, and discourse structure.

title: JPN 4415 Japanese Translation: Theory and Practice 3 Credits
description: Key concepts and approaches of translation studies applied to the translation of Japanese to English, and English to Japanese, in a variety of media, genre, and text types.

title: JPN 4850 Structure of Japanese 3 Credits
description: Linguistic analysis of modern standard Japanese. Topics include phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics and writing. Readings and discussions in English. (N)

title: JPN 4905 Individual Study 1-5 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course. Available only by special arrangement.

title: JPN 4911 Undergraduate Research in Language or Linguistics 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Language or Linguistics. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Language or Linguistics.

title: JPN 4930 Special Topics in Japanese Studies 3 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in and in-depth study of special topics in Japanese studies.

title: JPN 4935 Senior Honors Thesis 3 Credits
description: Select a Japanese faculty member to act as director for an independent research project that culminates in an honors thesis.

title: JPN 4940 Internship 0-6 Credits
description: Faculty (or delegated authority) supervised internship. Requires a written post-internship report.

title: JPN 4956 Overseas Studies 1 1-15 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: JPN 4957 Overseas Studies 2 1-15 Credits
description: Revolving topics provide a mechanism for coursework taken at a foreign university as part of an approved study abroad program to be transferred to UF. Credits taken will be entered in the student's transcript and may or may not count toward graduation, at the discretion of major's advisors.

title: JPT 3100 Tales of Kyoto 3 Credits
description: An investigation of literary texts from the 8th through the 17th centuries presented within the framework of Western literary and feminist criticism.

title: JPT 3120 Modern Japanese Fiction in Translation 3 Credits
description: Critical examination of stories, autobiographies, and secondary criticism from the 19th century to the present. Become familiar with the forms and topics of criticism through Japanese and Western sources.

title: JPT 3121 Contemporary Japanese Literature: Postwar to Postmodern 3 Credits
description: Companion to JPT 3120 that reflects the increasing clarity with which contemporary Japanese literature (1945 to present) is emerging as a separate field with its own set of issues, major texts and significance for the American student of Japan. Writers range from Dazai and Oe Kenzaburo to Murakami Haruki, and issues range from subjectivity to cybernetics.

title: JPT 3140 Modern Women Writers 3 Credits
description: Examination of narratives written by women who published during the Taisho (1912-25), Showa (1925-89) and Heisei (1989 to present) periods.

title: JPT 3150 Classical Japanese Poetry 3 Credits
description: Historical survey of traditional Japanese poetry (waka) from the 8th to the 16th century.

title: JPT 3300 Samurai War Tales 3 Credits
description: Explores the historical and cultural stimuli that led to war, recorded later as war narratives. Supported by images of architecture, narrative picture scrolls, and extant military accoutrements. (N)

title: JPT 3330 Early Modern Japanese Literature 3 Credits
description: Surveys Japanese literature of the Early Modern period (also called the Edo period), 1600-1868. Introduces and analyzes historically significant, foundational works of Early Modern Japanese literature and theater. Explores the history of the period and the development of print technologies and new genres, and introduces critical aesthetic and cultural concepts.

title: JPT 3391 Introduction to Japanese Film 4 Credits
description: Introduces the formal and historical features of Japanese film that have given it a unique position in film history. Emphasizes formal and critical analysis as well as the intellectual stakes of studying non-western film.

title: JPT 3500 Japanese Culture 3 Credits
description: Introduction to Japanese culture with emphasis on tracing the origin and development of important aspects of Japanese literature, art, religion, and society. All readings in English.

title: JPT 3702 Japanese Visual Culture 3 Credits
description: Explores issues within Japanese visual culture, and uses visual-cultural products to explore Japanese political, cultural, social, and historical issues. Introduces and analyzes historically significant, foundational works of Japanese visual culture, unpacks genre and genre tropes in contemporary works, and analyzes several visual-cultural works that tackle significant social issues.

title: JPT 4130 The Tale of Genji 3 Credits
description: Investigation of the 11th-century masterpiece and its pervasive influence on Japanese literature, past and present.

title: JPT 4502 Japanese Folklore 3 Credits
description: Study of native belief systems and the supernatural as reflected in the folk practice of ritual observance and in tales, myths, songs, and proverbs.

title: JPT 4510 Representations of Japan's Modern Empire 3 Credits
description: Examines a variety of literary, historical, anthropological, and theoretical texts to explore racial and social issues related to Japan's imperial past. (N)

title: JPT 4911 Undergraduate Research in English Translation 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: JPT 4956 Overseas Studies 1 1-15 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: JPT 4957 Overseas Studies 2 1-15 Credits
description: Revolving topics provide a mechanism for coursework taken at a foreign university as part of an approved study abroad program to be transferred to UF. Credits taken will be entered in the student's transcript and may or may not count toward graduation, at the discretion of major's advisors.

title: JPW 3143 Classical Japanese 1 3 Credits
description: Introduction to classical Japanese texts with emphasis on reading comprehension, grammar analysis and translation.

title: JPW 3144 Classical Japanese 2 3 Credits
description: Complex texts in classical Japanese with focus on comprehension, grammar, literature and culture.

title: JPW 4130 Readings in Japanese Literature 3 Credits
description: A fourth-year language course based on literary texts, incorporating advanced reading skills and the analysis of literature in the original. (N)

title: JPW 4131 Japanese Texts and Contexts 3 Credits
description: Complements JPW 4130, Readings in Japanese Literature, and focuses on contemporary issues as encountered in a variety of Japanese media.

title: JPW 4911 Undergraduate Research in Target Language 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Target Language. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Target Language.

title: KOR 1130 Beginning Korean 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering speaking, listening, reading, writing, and cultural interaction.

title: KOR 1131 Beginning Korean 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of speaking, listening, reading, writing and cultural interaction.

title: KOR 2230 Intermediate Korean 1 5 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: KOR 2231 Intermediate Korean 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: KOR 4930 Special Topics in Korean Studies 3 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in and in-depth study of special topics in Korean studies.

title: KOT 3500 Introduction to Korean Culture 3 Credits
description: Introduction to Korean culture with emphasis on tracing the origin and development of important aspects of Korean literature, art, religion, and society. Taught in English.

title: PLT 3930 Special Topics in Polish Studies 3 Credits
description: Variable topics in Polish literature, culture and society. Taught in English.

title: POL 1130 Introduction to Polish Language and Culture 1 5 Credits
description: A two-semester Polish language sequence that introduces the basics of Polish language and culture.

title: POL 1131 Introduction to Polish Language and Culture 2 5 Credits
description: Second in the two-semester sequence. Helps to expand vocabulary, command of Polish grammar, and the ability to speak Polish.

title: POL 2220 Intermediate Polish 1 4 Credits
description: Improve speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills by reviewing and expanding the language principles introduced in POL 1130 and POL 1131.

title: POL 2221 Intermediate Polish 2 4 Credits
description: Improve speaking, reading, writing, and listening comprehension skills by reviewing and building upon the language principles introduced in POL 2220.

title: RUS 1130 Introduction to Russian Language and Culture 1 5 Credits
description: RUS 1130 and its sequel, RUS 1131, offer a comprehensive introduction to Russian, using interactive methods to develop competence in speaking, listening, reading, writing and cultural interaction.

title: RUS 1131 Introduction to Russian Language and Culture 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of introductory language and cultural study.

title: RUS 2220 Intermediate Russian 1 4 Credits
description: Intermediate study with exercises in sentence patterns, vocabulary building and oral and written discourse in Russian.

title: RUS 2340 Russian for Heritage Learners 3 Credits
description: A practical overview of Russian grammar and writing for those with significant bilingual speaking and listening backgrounds. Devotes special attention to reading, writing and vocabulary development.

title: RUS 3240 Oral Practice in Russian 3 Credits
description: Development of advanced speaking and listening skills in conversational Russian.

title: RUS 3400 Intermediate Russian 2 4 Credits
description: Continued study with exercises in sentence patterns, vocabulary building and sustained oral and written discourse.

title: RUS 4000 Advanced Russian 1 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills (speaking, listening, reading, and writing) with attention to more complex structures.

title: RUS 4001 Advanced Russian 2 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills (speaking, listening, reading, and writing) with attention to more complex structures.

title: RUS 4300 Advanced Grammar and Composition 3 Credits
description: Study of advanced grammar and composition in Russian.

title: RUS 4411 Advanced Oral Practice 3 Credits
description: Continued development of advanced speaking and listening skills based on authentic written, audio and video texts from contemporary Russian culture.

title: RUS 4501 Russian Studies Research Seminar 3 Credits
description: Introduces significant trends and ideas in Russian literary, cultural, historical, and critical studies. Develop the ability to understand and produce critical scholarly argument in a variety of formats, including class discussion, formal presentation, and a written research project.

title: RUS 4502 Language and Culture of the Russian Business World 3 Credits
description: Combines advanced language training, a practical introduction to the language and culture of the contemporary Russian business world, and extensive practice translating and interpreting texts used in business settings. Focuses on issues such as starting companies and joint ventures, advertising, and setting up and conducting official meetings and telephone calls. Basic Russian business ethics are also studied.

title: RUS 4503 Theory and Practice of Russian-English Translation 1 3 Credits
description: First part of a two-part translation series focusing on the theory and practice of conveying word semantics in Russian-English and English-Russian translation.

title: RUS 4504 Theory and Practice of Russian-English Translation 2 3 Credits
description: Second part of the translation series focusing on the theory and practice of conveying sentence and paragraph semantics in Russian-English and English-Russian translation.

title: RUS 4700 Structure of the Russian Language 3 Credits
description: Introduction to the phonology, morphology and syntax of contemporary standard Russian from a formal and semantic standpoint. Also includes the development of Russian word stress and a survey of the development of Russian verb tense, aspect and mood.

title: RUS 4780 Corrective Phonetics and Intonation 3 Credits
description: Develops advanced-level phonetic and intonational skills via a variety of genres of authentic texts from Russian culture.

title: RUS 4905 Individual Work in Russian 1-3 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course. Available only by special arrangement.

title: RUS 4911 Undergraduate Research in Russian Language 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research in Russian Language. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Russian Language.

title: RUS 4930 Special Topics in Russian 3-9 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in Russian studies. New, experimental, or one-time offerings.

title: RUS 4956 Overseas Studies 1-15 Credits
description: Provides a mechanism by which coursework taken as part of an approved study abroad program can be recorded on the UF transcript and counted toward graduation.

title: RUT 3101 Russian Masterpieces 3 Credits
description: Introduces the Russian literature of the 19th-21st centuries. Read some of the most influential works by Pushkin, Gogol, Tolstoy, Dostoevsky, Chekhov, Nabokov, Pasternak and Sorokin, thereby gaining essential knowledge of Russian history, culture and the authors personal lives. Readings and discussions in English. (N)

title: RUT 3441 Tolstoy and Dostoevsky 3 Credits
description: Introduction to the major 19th century Russian novelists and their contemporaries. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 3442 Themes from Russian Literature 3 Credits
description: Examines Russian everyday life and institutions of the 19th and 20th centuries through the media of literature and film.

title: RUT 3443 War and Peace 3 Credits
description: Offers a close reading of Tolstoys epic novel War and Peace. Examines the literary and historical origins of the novel, its reflection of Tolstoy's obsession with history, and the emerging picture it presents of Russian identity. Taught in English.

title: RUT 3452 Russian Literature of the Twentieth Century 3 Credits
description: Authors, movements, and genres in Russian literature from the Revolution of 1917 to the present. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 3500 Russian Cultural Heritage 3 Credits
description: Introduces the culture of pre-revolutionary Russia. Philosophical, religious, artistic, and literary currents in relation to Western civilization. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 3501 Contemporary Russian Culture and Society 3 Credits
description: Patterns of continuity and change in the philosophical and cultural values of Russian society as they explain the Soviet Union and contemporary Russia. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 3503 Violence and Terror in the Russian Experience 3 Credits
description: Examines the impact of violence and terror on the human condition as reflected and expressed in well-known works of Russian literature (fiction and historical narratives), art and film. Taught in English. No knowledge of other languages required.

title: RUT 3504 Russia Today 3 Credits
description: Patterns of continuity and change in the philosophical and cultural values of Russian society as they explain the Soviet Union and contemporary Russia. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 3506 Creative Lives: Writers, Artists, and Extraordinary People 3 Credits
description: Explores Russian visions of creativity and creative people in Russian culture by placing them in dialogue with broader European and American models and considerations of creativity.

title: RUT 3514 Russian Fairy Tales 3 Credits
description: Critical introduction to Russian fairy tales and folklore and an examination of the aesthetic, social, cultural, and psychological values they reflect.

title: RUT 3524 Russia through Film 3 Credits
description: Examines Russian life, institutions, and the arts against the background of period-defining historical, political, and cultural events from the early twentieth century to the present day as reflected and refracted in some emblematic feature films, documentaries, and animated cartoon films produced in the Soviet Union and post-Soviet Russia.

title: RUT 3530 Russia's Struggle with Nature: Legacies of Destruction and Preservation 3 Credits
description: Explores competing concepts of nature in modern Russian culture through works of Russian fiction and non-fiction in translation. Emphasis falls on environmental problems in Russia and the former Soviet Union (desiccation of the Aral Sea, pollution of Lake Baikal, the Chernobyl nuclear accident etc.) and their impact on Russian thought.

title: RUT 3600 The Twentieth Century through Slavic Eyes 3 Credits
description: Introduces the literature, film, and culture of 20th century Eastern and Central Europe.

title: RUT 3601 The Culture of the Cold War 3 Credits
description: Examines Soviet and American cultural responses to the Cold War across a wide range of media (literature, film, propaganda). Consideration of how relations between the US and the USSR were framed as a global ideological contest.

title: RUT 3930 Variable Topics in Russian Studies 1-9 Credits
description: Variable topics in Russian studies, taught in English translation.

title: RUT 4440 Pushkin and Gogol 3 Credits
description: Major works of Russian literature written in the first half of the 19th century. Readings and discussions in English.

title: RUT 4450 Russian Modernism 3 Credits
description: Introduces the major artistic contributions of Russian modernism in the context of the political, social, and cultural upheavals of late Imperial to Stalinist Russia (1890 - 1939).

title: RUT 4911 Undergraduate Research in Russian Studies, English Translation 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: RUT 4930 Variable Topics in Russian Studies 1-9 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in Russian studies, taught in English translation.

title: RUW 3101 Reading Russian Literature 3 Credits
description: In-depth study of the vocabulary, structures, reading strategies and cultural background needed for understanding and interpreting a broad range of Russian prose fiction.

title: RUW 4301 Russian Drama and Poetry 3-6 Credits
description: Study of plays and poems by major Russian writers from the 19th and 20th centuries. Readings in Russian.

title: RUW 4341 Russian Media Culture 3 Credits
description: In-depth study of the history and recent trends in the Russian mass media and web-based technology and their impact on culture and society.

title: RUW 4370 Russian Short Prose 3 Credits
description: Critical study of selected Russian short stories. Readings in Russian.

title: RUW 4630 Reading Eugene Onegin: Pushkin and Nabokov 3 Credits
description: An in-depth study of one of Russia's most revered literary works, combining close readings of Pushkin's original with analyses of operatic, artistic and cinematic adaptations by Chaikovsky, Nabokov and others. Readings and discussions primarily in Russian.

title: RUW 4911 Undergraduate Research in Russian Studies, Target Language 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: RUW 4932 Selected Readings in Russian 1-3 Credits
description: Special topic, author, genre, or movement in Russian literature.

title: SSW 3303 Swahili Oral Literature 3 Credits
description: Introduces various genres of Swahili oral literatures and shows their importance, relevance, and function within Swahili culture.

title: SWA 1130 Beginning Swahili 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering four skills: listening, speaking, reading and writing.

title: SWA 1131 Beginning Swahili 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: SWA 2200 Intermediate Swahili 1 3 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: SWA 2201 Intermediate Swahili 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: SWA 3410 Advanced Swahili 1 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: SWA 3411 Advanced Swahili 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: SWA 4905 Individual Study 1-5 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course.

title: SWW 4100 Readings in Swahili 3 Credits
description: Readings in Swahili

title: VTN 1130 Beginning Vietnamese 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering four skills: listening, speaking, reading and writing.

title: VTN 1131 Beginning Vietnamese 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: VTN 2220 Intermediate Vietnamese 1 4 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: VTN 2221 Intermediate Vietnamese 2 4 Credits
description: Continuation of intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: VTN 2340 Vietnamese for Heritage Learners 1 4 Credits
description: For those with significant speaking and listening skills, but limited reading and writing skills. Emphasis on developing latter two skills.

title: VTN 4905 Individual Study 1-5 Credits
description: For independent work not offered in another course. Available only by special arrangement.

title: VTN 4930 Special Topics in Vietnamese Studies 3 Credits
description: Variable topics dealing with specific issues in Vietnamese studies. Available only by special arrangement.

title: VTT 3500 Vietnamese Culture 3 Credits
description: Overview of Vietnamese culture, language and history. Focuses on Vietnamese cultural norms, attitudes, values and culture through an examination of history, religious practices, language, literature, family structures, cuisine, daily life and the arts. (N)

title: WOL 1130 Beginning Wolof 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering four skills: listening, speaking, reading and writing.

title: WOL 1131 Beginning Wolof 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: WOL 2200 Intermediate Wolof 1 3 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: WOL 2201 Intermediate Wolof 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of intermediate study.

title: WOL 3410 Advanced Wolof 1 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: WOL 3411 Advanced Wolof 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: SSA 3730 Language in African Society 3 Credits
description: The role of language in the development of African societies. Language and nation building.

title: YOR 1130 Beginning Yoruba 1 5 Credits
description: Beginning study covering four skills: listening, speaking, reading and writing.

title: YOR 1131 Beginning Yoruba 2 5 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: YOR 2200 Intermediate Yoruba 1 3 Credits
description: Intermediate study of the four skills with new vocabulary and grammar.

title: YOR 2201 Intermediate Yoruba 2 3 Credits
description: Continued study of the four skills with additional vocabulary and grammar.

title: YOR 3410 Advanced Yoruba 1 3 Credits
description: Advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: YOR 3411 Advanced Yoruba 2 3 Credits
description: Continuation of advanced study of the four skills with attention to more complex structures.

title: YOR 4905 Individual Study 1-5 Credits
description: For those who seek independent work not offered in another course.

title: YOT 3500 Yoruba Diaspora in the New World 3 Credits
description: Introduction to the scope and importance of Yoruban culture in the New World and its role and significance in the resilience of African cultures in North America, South America and the Caribbean. (WR)

title: YRW 4130 Readings in Yoruba Literature 3 Credits
description: Reading skills and the analysis of literature in the original language.

title: LAS 1141 Human Rights in Latin America 3 Credits
description: Explores the historical development of the idea of human rights with a focus on Latin America using an interdisciplinary approach grounded in the humanities and social sciences. Students develop their own ethnographic research using qualitative and interpretive methods to gather evidence and reach reasoned conclusions.

title: LAS 2001 Introduction to Latin American Studies 3 Credits
description: Interdisciplinary introduction to the study of Latin American societies and cultures. Provides general knowledge of Latin America and its people, preparing students for future coursework in Latin American studies. (N)

title: LAS 3040 Drug Wars and Oil Fortunes in Latin America 3 Credits
description: Examines the historical, comparative, and current dynamics of two global commodities—illicit drugs and licit petroleum. Considers how the presumably separate spheres of legal and illegal commodity exchange have become intertwined and covers the linkages between the Drug War, petroleum extractivism, and United States—Latin America relations.

title: LAS 3930 Special Topics in Latin American Studies 3 Credits
description: Variable topics in Latin American studies.

title: LAS 4005 Latin American Thought 3 Credits
description: Latin American Thought is designed to offer students from all majors a comprehensive overview, as well as an in-depth discussion, of the most influential Latin American thinkers and their works.

title: LAS 4010 Reproduction and Reproductive Justice in the Americas 3 Credits
description: Examines the history of reproduction as a political and social phenomenon in the Americas, including North, South, and Central America, and the Caribbean. Topics covered include eugenics, reproduction under slavery, population control, reproductive rights activism, adoption, surrogacy, and reproductive technologies as a means of understanding how societies and governments regulate belonging, citizenship, physical and moral fitness, and gendered behaviors.

title: LAS 4011 Social Movements and Gender: A View from Latin America 3 Credits
description: This seminar examines social and cultural movements led by women and LGBTQ activists in twentieth-century Latin America. Analyze the major moments of social and political change in the region through the prisms of gender, race, and sexuality, asking how these transitions were experienced by women and people of non-normative genders and sexualities, problematizing and disaggregating these categories.

title: LAS 4012 Consumption and Social Change in Latin America 3 Credits
description: Examines consumption patterns in Latin America and among Latinx communities in the US, situating them within processes of globalization, identity formation, and social inequality. Analyze key sectors such as food, fashion, technology, leisure, and housing, exploring how cultural, political, and environmental dynamics shape practices of consumption. Critically uses case studies and creative methodologies, including netnography, video, mapping, and visual tools.

title: LAS 4905 Individual Work 1-3 Credits
description: Qualified students choose a topic in Latin American studies that cuts across disciplines. Students work under the supervision of a faculty member affiliated to the Center for Latin American Studies.

title: LAS 4911 Undergraduate Research in Latin American Studies 0-3 Credits
description: Course provides firsthand, supervised research in Latin American Studies. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery or application in Latin American Studies.

title: LAS 4935 Latin American Area Seminar 3 Credits
description: Multidisciplinary variable topics seminar. Required of all candidates for the undergraduate certificate and minor in Latin American studies. (WR)

title: POS 4074 Latino Politics and Policy 3 Credits
description: Examines the political behavior of Hispanic Americans in the United States. Students discuss political and public policy issues that impact Hispanic/Latino communities, such as affirmative action, the Chicano movement, English-only movements, immigration, the presidency, racial profiling and urban politics.

title: LAT 1101 Beginning Latin 2 3 Credits
description: The second part of the sequence for those with little or no background in Latin.

title: LAT 1104 Beginning Latin 3 3 Credits
description: The third part of the sequence for students with little background in high-school Latin.

title: LAT 1120 Beginning Latin 1 4 Credits
description: The first of a 3-semester sequence for students with little or no background in Latin. Others enrolling in the course will be required to take it for an S/U grade.

title: LAT 1130 Accelerated Beginning Latin 1 5 Credits
description: This course and its sequel, LAT 1131, constitute the basic sequence for development of overall skill in the language. Students are expected students to have little or no background in Latin. Others enrolling in the course will be required to take it for an S/U grade.

title: LAT 1131 Accelerated Beginning Latin 2 5 Credits
description: Continuation of the basic sequence for development of overall skill in the language.

title: LIT 2000 Introduction to Literature 3 Credits
description: In this course, students will be assigned readings representative of a broad range of literary genres and cultures. These readings will cover a variety of literary movements and historical eras. The readings will include selections from the Western canon. Written analysis of literary works may be required. Students will be provided with opportunities to practice critical interpretation.

title: LNW 2321 Introduction to Vergil 3 Credits
description: Readings in Vergil's Eclogues, Georgics and/or the Aeneid, with emphasis on introducing Vergilian style, diction poetic techniques and basic genre differences. Review of Latin grammar and syntax.

title: LNW 2560 Readings in Latin Literature 3 Credits
description: Examines various aspects of Roman life through readings in Latin literature (with a focus on either special subjects, authors, genres or periods) and a review of Latin grammar.

title: LNW 2630 Latin Love Poetry 3 Credits
description: Translation and interpretation of selected poems of Catullus and a thorough review of Latin grammar.

title: LNW 3220 The Ancient Novel 3 Credits
description: Readings from Petronius, Apuleius or the Historia Apollonii Regis Tyri.

title: LNW 3310 Roman Drama 3 Credits
description: Translation and analysis of the comedies of Plautus and Terence or the tragedies of Seneca.

title: LNW 3320 Roman Elegy and Lyric 3 Credits
description: Selected poems of Catullus, Horace, Tibullus, Propertius or Ovid. (N)

title: LNW 3360 Roman Satire 3 Credits
description: Translation and analysis of the Roman satirists Horace, Persius, Juvenal, or Martial.

title: LNW 3380 The Roman Historians 3 Credits
description: Readings in Latin from one of the Roman historians: Sallust, Caesar, Livy, or Tacitus.

title: LNW 3490 Medieval Latin 3 Credits
description: Readings from Medieval Latin 350 - 1200 A.D.

title: LNW 3644 Cicero 3 Credits
description: Essays, speeches and letters of Cicero. (N)

title: LNW 3660 Vergil and Roman Epic 3 Credits
description: Translation and analysis of selections from Vergil's Aeneid in the light of his epic techniques and the spirit of the Augustan Age.

title: LNW 3930 Studies in Latin Literature 3 Credits
description: A rotating topics course providing in-depth study of a particular author (e.g., Suetonius), genre (e.g., didactic poetry) or period (e.g., The Silver Age).

title: LNW 4905 Special Study in Latin 1-4 Credits
description: Readings, conferences and reports.

title: LNW 4911 Undergraduate Research in Latin Language and Literature 0-3 Credits
description: Provides firsthand, supervised research. Projects may involve inquiry, design, investigation, scholarship, discovery, or application.

title: LIN 1140 Language and Emotion 3 Credits
description: Interdisciplinary study of the interface of emotion and language. Focus on vocal properties of emotion expression, how different languages conceptualize emotion, how those who are not neurotypical may differ in the expression of emotion, and implications of gender-differential expectations of emotion expression for self-presentation.

title: LIN 2004 Languages of the World 3 Credits
description: Broad survey of the world’s languages, their genetic affiliations, and current status. Addresses issues related to linguistic diversity and provides an overview of the major subfields of linguistics by acquainting students with linguistic properties of diverse languages and language families.

title: LIN 2008 Language and Computers 3 Credits
description: Identifies and explains the cross-disciplinary dimensions of AI for natural language processing (NLP) through social science principles of human communication (e.g. conversational turn-taking), linguistic theory (e.g. how to build sentences), and computer science (e.g. ASCII vs. Unicode). Examines topics in the context of globalization, language endangerment, and the recent rapid rise of AI. Assumes no background in linguistics or computer science.

title: LIN 2011 Introduction to Linguistics 3 Credits
description: Explores the nature of human language. Provides a broad overview of the different fields of linguistics including phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, pragmatics, sociolinguistics, and historical linguistics (which will include a discussion of texts from the Western canon) to explore the myriad ways in which linguists analyze human language phenomena.

title: LIN 2141 Linguistic Prejudice 3 Credits
description: Addresses the question “what we can do to mitigate the effects of Linguistic Prejudice?” Explores the meaning of linguistic prejudice, who it affects, and its real-world consequences: discrimination in education, housing, and the justice system. Draws from linguistics, psychology and sociology; investigates how to reduce linguistic prejudice and its effects.

title: LIN 2614 Language in the USA 3 Credits
description: Focuses on linguistic diversity in the US, touching on American dialects, multilingualism in America, Native American languages, and sociolinguistic variation. Dialect analysis tools will be learned and applied in class projects.

title: LIN 2704 Language, Thought and Action: Language as a Cognitive System 3 Credits
description: What you know about language and how to use it when listening and speaking resides in your mind. Language is therefore part of human cognition. One important part of understanding language and human linguistic behavior is to understand how language interacts with other aspects of cognition: thought, perception, emotion and action. In this course will discuss recent insights in language and the mind. In this course will discuss recent insights in language and the mind from various disciplines including linguistics, cognitive psychology, neuroscience and computer science. This course counts toward the social and behavioral sciences requirement by exploring this relationship between mind and language, in other words covering the cognitive aspects of language.

title: LIN 3201 The Sounds of Human Language 3 Credits
description: Emphasizes the sounds employed in languages of the world for a comprehensive understanding of the mechanism underlying the production of speech sounds and the ability to recognize, distinguish and phonetically transcribe speech sounds from an unfamiliar language. Includes investigation of the patterning and functions of sounds in languages of the world and application of the methods of analyzing a language sound system.

title: LIN 3460 The Structure of Human Language 3 Credits
description: Investigates word formation processes (morphology) and sentence structure (syntax) in the world's languages. Focuses on vocabulary and tools used to discover and describe patterns in human language. Coursework emphasizes problem solving and working with a wide range of language data.

title: LIN 3677 World Englishes 3 Credits
description: Investigates history and spread of English; varieties of English spoken around the world as a first, second, or foreign language, their characteristics at various linguistic levels (sounds, morphemes, syntax, lexicon), their use and status in different countries; issues of policy, teaching, and testing; and models categorizing World Englishes.

title: LIN 3680 Modern English Structure 3 Credits
description: Studies the grammar of current English from the viewpoint of modern linguistics.

title: LIN 4005 Stats for Linguists 3 Credits
description: Introduces the concepts of probability and statistics, with examples chosen mainly from linguistics. Topics include descriptive statistics, comparing means, regression, t-tests, linear mixed models, and basic experimental design.

title: LIN 4071 Intro to Corpus Linguistics 3 Credits
description: Key methods of corpus linguistics for research and teaching.

title: LIN 4124 Historical Linguistics 3 Credits
description: Basic concepts of historical linguistics and their application to language data. Exploration of the history of individual languages and language families. Concepts of language relatedness, language change, and language evolution.

title: LIN 4136 Introduction to Data-driven Learning 3 Credits
description: An overview of how data-driven learning is defined and what disciplines and theories it draws from, the way DDL has been applied in actual classroom research, and hands-on training using software and corpus data to develop instructional materials and student activities.

title: LIN 4205 Fundamentals of Phonetics 3 Credits
description: Studies phonetics, including anatomy and physiology of the speech production apparatus, transcription and production of some of the world's sounds, basic acoustics, computerized methods for speech analysis, acoustic characteristics of sounds, stress and intonation and basic issues in speech perception. May be taught in conjunction with LIN 6208, Phonetics and Linguists, a graduate course.

title: LIN 4320 Introduction to Phonology 3 Credits
description: Phonemics, syllabic and prosodic phenomena, neutralization, distinctive features, morphophonemic alternation, phonological systems and processes. Terminology and notational conventions of generative phonology and optimality theory, with problems from a variety of languages. May be taught in conjunction with a graduate course that bears the same title.

title: LIN 4400 Introduction to Morphology 3 Credits
description: Theory of word structure, derivation and inflection, with examples and problems from a variety of languages. Includes the position of morphology in grammar, the relationship between morphology and grammar, typology, cultural and conceptual categories, and predictions of various theories of morphology. May be taught in conjunction with a graduate course that bears the same title.

title: LIN 4500 Introduction to Syntax 3 Credits
description: Generative-transformational model of syntax: phrase structure, the lexicon, case and agreement, movement, locality relations and anaphora; emphasizes problem solving and linguistic argumentation. May be taught in conjunction with a graduate course that bears the same title.

title: LIN 4600 Survey of Sociolinguistics 3 Credits
description: Major approaches to language in context: ethnolinguistic, sociological and linguistic. Applications of socio-linguistics to applied linguistics, social sciences and education. Collection and analysis of data.

title: LIN 4656 Gender and Language 3 Credits
